content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6-max

## Summary

The amendment correctly identifies that the two stale-replay fast paths (lines 246-270 and 298-320) return before reaching the shared tracing::warn!/tracing::error! block at lines 409-448. Both paths log only at info level without the error_summary field. Critically, predicted_outcome can be EscalatedToOperator (when attempt_number >= MAX_FAILURE_RETRIES), meaning operator escalations from replay paths are silently logged at info level instead of error level. This is a real observability gap that would make partial-write recovery scenarios harder to detect in production.

## Votes

- `fr-15-7fabe6a3`: Accept
  Verified in the source: both stale-replay fast paths (lines 246-270 and 298-320) return Ok(predicted_outcome) after only a tracing::info! call that lacks the error_summary structured field. The shared tracing::warn! (line 409, includes error_summary) and tracing::error! (line 440, for escalations) are never reached by these paths. Since failure_outcome() can return EscalatedToOperator when attempt_number >= MAX_FAILURE_RETRIES, a replayed failure that triggers operator escalation would only appear as info-level — a genuine observability defect. The remediation (emit warn/error telemetry before early returns, or refactor to flow through the common block) is straightforward and correct.
